content.problemStatement:
You are given a **binary** string SSS of length NNN.

You can apply the following operation any (possibly zero) number of times:


Select an integer i (1‚â§i‚â§N‚àí2)i\ \lparen 1 \leq i \leq N-2 \rpareni (1‚â§i‚â§N‚àí2) and replace the substring SiSi+1Si+2S_i S_{i+1} S_{i+2}Si‚ÄãSi+1‚ÄãSi+2‚Äã with `100`.
Find the **lexicographically smallest** string after the operations.

Note: String XXX is lexicographically smaller than string YYY if Xi<YiX_i \lt Y_iXi‚Äã<Yi‚Äã, where iii is the first index where XXX and YYY differ.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN ‚Äî the length of string SSS.
The second line of each test case contains a binary string SSS.




Output Format
For each test case, output the **lexicographically smallest** string after the operations.
constraints: Each character of SSS is either `0` or `1`.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3‚ãÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: You can convert SSS into 100.
Since 100 is lexicographically smaller than 110, output 100.
Test case 222: You should keep SSS as it is without applying any operations, so output 0001.
